description: Study 1: main A/B sweep. 19 Putnam real-analysis problems x 2 conditions (A = lean-lsp only; B = lean-lsp + math-viz) x 3 runs = 120 unattended 'claude --print' formalize-and-prove attempts, scored by build_ok, sorry-count, and an LLM statement-match judge.
motivation: Operationalize the root question directly: if visualization helps, condition B should solve more, especially on graph-friendly (integral/asymptotic) subtopics.
formal_results: A: 60 runs, 19 solved (31.7%), 58 build_ok; B: 60 runs, 17 solved (28.3%), 57 build_ok. Gap within +/-12pt 95% CI (noise). In all 60 B runs the model issued ZERO tool_use blocks for any math-viz tool. ~2/3 of attempts ended build-ok with exactly one sorry.
results_description: No measurable difference in solve rate, and the visualization treatment was never invoked - the sweep measured whether the model CHOOSES viz (it does not), not whether viz helps.
conclusions: Voluntary adoption of math-viz is zero under optional advertisement; the A/B contrast is uninformative about tool value because the treatment was never delivered.

comments.provenance: The 120-run raw dataset is no longer in the repo; the 31.7%/28.3% solve numbers are quoted from experiments/REPORT.md, not re-derivable from results/ (only a 6-dir later sample remains).
